targets.query:
from(bucket: "nesquic")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "nesquic" and r._field == "throughput")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.library == "noq")

options.content: This experiment runs the client and server in a separate network namespace, but does alter their traffic in any way.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "nesquic")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "nesquic_io" and r._field == "volume_kb_sum")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.library == "noq" and r.mode == "server" and r.job == "unbounded")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "syscall"])
  |> rename(columns: {"_value": "count"})
  |> group()

from(bucket: "nesquic")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "nesquic_io" and r._field == "volume_kb_sum")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.library == "noq" and r.mode == "client" and r.job == "unbounded")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "syscall"])
  |> rename(columns: {"_value": "count"})
  |> group()

options.content: This experiment simulates a cell phone that is connected to an LTE network while driving. 50ms delay are applied to the traffic.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "nesquic")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "nesquic_io" and r._field == "volume_kb_sum")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.library == "noq" and r.mode == "server" and r.job == "driving")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "syscall"])
  |> rename(columns: {"_value": "count"})
  |> group()
